title: thymus serpyllum f. glaber
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. glaber, also known as glabrous wild thyme, is a subspecies of the wild thyme plant, characterized by its hairless leaves and stems. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of glabrous wild thyme include reducing stress and anxiety, improving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and boosting the immune system. It can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
